input.command: cat prompt.txt; echo "===PRECOND==="; cat precondition.log; echo "===KUBECTL==="; which kubectl; kubectl version --short 2>/dev/null | head

content.content:
A 3-member MongoDB replica set is running in the mongodb namespace.
Each member is exposed via NodePort with external hostnames:

- domain-rs-1:31181
- domain-rs-2:31182
- domain-rs-3:31183

A mongo-client pod is available with those hostnames mapped to a node IP.

Task:
- Configure the replica set to advertise the external endpoints (split-horizon).
- Do not change the NodePort services.===PRECOND===
[precondition:mongo_env_ready:preconditionUnits[0] 'mongo_env_ready':probe] $ kubectl -n mongodb get pod mongo-rs-0 2>/dev/null | grep -c Running
0

[precondition:mongo_env_ready:preconditionUnits[0] 'mongo_env_ready':apply] $ /bin/sh -c '
kubectl delete namespace mongodb --ignore-not-found=true; kubectl wait --for=delete namespace/mongodb --timeout=120s || true
'


[precondition:mongo_env_ready:preconditionUnits[0] 'mongo_env_ready':apply] $ kubectl create namespace mongodb
namespace/mongodb created

[precondition:mongo_env_ready:preconditionUnits[0] 'mongo_env_ready':apply] $ kubectl -n mongodb apply -f cases/mongodb/external-access-horizons/resource/services.yaml
service/mongo created
service/mongo-external-0 created
service/mongo-external-1 created
service/mongo-external-2 created

[precondition:mongo_env_ready:preconditionUnits[0] 'mongo_env_ready':apply] $ kubectl -n mongodb apply -f cases/mongodb/external-access-horizons/resource/statefulset.yaml
statefulset.apps/mongo-rs created

[precondition:mongo_env_ready:preconditionUnits[0] 'mongo_env_ready':apply] $ for i in $(seq 1 60); do
  ready=$(kubectl -n mongodb get sts mongo-rs -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}' 2>/dev/null)
  if [ "${ready:-0}" -ge 3 ]; then
    exit 0
  fi
  sleep 5
done
exit 1


[precondition:mongo_env_ready:preconditionUnits[0] 'mongo_env_ready':apply] $ kubectl -n mongodb exec mongo-rs-0 -- mongosh --quiet --eval '
rs.initiate({_id:"rs0",members:[
  {_id:0,host:"mongo-rs-0.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017"},
  {_id:1,host:"mongo-rs-1.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017"},
  {_id:2,host:"mongo-rs-2.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017"}
]})'

{ ok: 1 }

[precondition:mongo_env_ready:preconditionUnits[0] 'mongo_env_ready':apply] $ for i in $(seq 1 20); do
  kubectl -n mongodb exec mongo-rs-0 -- mongosh --quiet --eval 'db.hello().isWritablePrimary' | grep true && exit 0
  sleep 3
done
exit 1

true

[precondition:mongo_env_ready:preconditionUnits[0] 'mongo_env_ready':apply] $ node_ip=$(kubectl get nodes -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{" "}{range .status.addresses[?(@.type=="InternalIP")]}{.address}{"\n"}{end}{end}' | awk '$1 !~ /control-plane|master/ {print $2; exit}')
if [ -z "$node_ip" ]; then
  # Single-node cluster (only the control-plane node, e.g. default
  # kind): fall back to its InternalIP instead of failing.
  node_ip=$(kubectl get nodes -o jsonpath='{.items[0].status.addresses[?(@.type=="InternalIP")].address}')
fi
if [ -z "$node_ip" ]; then
  exit 1
fi
cat <<EOF | kubectl -n mongodb apply -f -
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: mongo-client
spec:
  restartPolicy: Never
  hostAliases:
  - ip: "$node_ip"
    hostnames:
    - domain-rs-1
    - domain-rs-2
    - domain-rs-3
  containers:
  - name: mongosh
    image: mongo:6.0
    command: ["sleep", "3600"]
EOF

pod/mongo-client created

[precondition:mongo_env_ready:preconditionUnits[0] 'mongo_env_ready':apply] $ kubectl -n mongodb wait --for=condition=ready pod mongo-client --timeout=300s
pod/mongo-client condition met

[precondition:mongo_env_ready:preconditionUnits[0] 'mongo_env_ready':verify] $ kubectl -n mongodb get pods 2>/dev/null | grep -c Running
4

[precondition:external_access_fixture:preconditionUnits[1] 'external_access_fixture':probe] $ kubectl -n mongodb get svc mongo-external-1
NAME               TYPE       CLUSTER-IP      EXTERNAL-IP   PORT(S)           AGE
mongo-external-1   NodePort   10.96.137.199   <none>        27017:31182/TCP   48s

===KUBECTL===
/users/Shaw_Liu/bin/kubectl

content.text: Now I understand the setup. The replica set uses internal hostnames; I need to add split-horizon `horizons` mappings to each member. Let me check the current config.

input.description: Get current replica set member config

content.text: No horizons configured yet. I'll add split-horizon mappings to each member via `rs.reconfig`, keeping the internal `host` values unchanged.
Reconfig succeeded. Now let me verify the horizons are set and that the NodePort services are unchanged, then test external connectivity from the mongo-client pod.

input.description: Verify horizons, services, and external connectivity
